Expiration Date: The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein.

Payment Terms: After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. Termination: A party may immediately terminate this Agreement by providing written notice: (a) if the other party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure that breach within sixty (60) days after receiving written notice of the breach, or (b) as provided in Sections 2.2, 2.4, or 12.4.

Party Names: bw-3 FRANCHISE SYSTEMS, INC. and an unspecified franchisee ("you")
Expiration Date: The term of this Agreement is for ten (10) years commencing on the date of this Agreement, unless terminated as provided by this Agreement.

Payment Terms: Initial Franchise Fee: Unspecified amount (blank in contract) payable on the date of the Agreement. Continuing Fee: Monthly fee equal to 5% of Gross Sales. Advertising and Development Fund Contribution: 3% of Gross Sales. Local Advertising: Up to 2% of Gross Sales. Grand Opening Advertising: Up to $5,000. Late payments bear interest up to 1.5% per month. Transfer Fee: One-half of the then-current Initial Franchise Fee. Temporary Operation Service Fee: Not less than $200 per day plus expenses if the Franchisor temporarily operates the restaurant due to Franchisee's default or absence.
Termination: The Franchisor may terminate the Agreement immediately for cause upon written notice if the Franchisee: fails to complete training; makes material misrepresentations; is convicted of a felony; makes unauthorized disclosures of confidential info; abandons the restaurant; makes unauthorized transfers; understates fees; becomes insolvent; misuses Marks or software; repeatedly fails to submit reports or pay fees; violates health/safety laws; fails to open within 9 months; or defaults on the lease. Termination is also permitted after notice and cure periods for failure to pay fees (10 days cure) or failure to comply with other provisions (30 days cure). The Franchisee may terminate if the Franchisor materially breaches and fails to cure within a reasonable time (at least 30 days).
Governing Law: The laws of the state in which the Franchised Restaurant is located, except to the extent governed by the United States Trademark Act of 1946 (Lanham Act).
Penalties: We shall give you written notice of our election not to renew the franchise at least three (3) months prior to the expiration of the initial or first renewal term of this Agreement.
